projectDescription: RedEye is a visual analytic tool developed by CISA for Red and Blue Teams to visualize, annotate, and report Command & Control (C2) operations from logs such as Cobalt Strike and Brute Ratel.
keyFeatures: Visual graph of C2 network showing beacons, hosts, and team servers
Red Team mode for uploading and annotating C2 campaign logs
Blue Team mode for read-only review of exported campaign data
Support for Cobalt Strike, Brute Ratel, and Sliver C2 framework parsers
Custom parser API for integrating any C2 log format
Interactive timeline for replaying attack sequences
Comment and tagging system with MITRE ATT&CK technique mapping
Presentation mode for communicating findings to stakeholders
Full-text search across beacons, hosts, operators, commands, and tags
Campaign export as portable .redeye files for Blue Team handoff
Anonymization support for redacting sensitive data before export
publicApiSurface: GraphQL API at /graphql (queries and mutations for campaigns, beacons, hosts, commands, annotations)
REST POST /upload-campaign — Upload C2 log files for parsing
REST GET /download-campaign — Export campaign as .redeye file
REST POST /login — Authenticate and create user session
REST GET /app-metadata — Retrieve server configuration metadata
Parser CLI: <parser> info — Returns parser configuration JSON
Parser CLI: <parser> parse-campaign --folder <path> — Parses C2 logs and returns structured JSON
